title: SQL Databases – Using SQLite3
description: Connect to a SQLite database and run raw SQL queries.

slides.title: SQLite – A Lightweight SQL Database 🗂️
slides.content:
# SQLite

SQLite is a self-contained, serverless, zero-configuration SQL database engine. It stores the entire database as a single file – great for prototyping, small apps, or local storage.

Install the Node.js driver:
```bash
npm install sqlite3
```

We'll use the `sqlite3` package, which provides both a low-level API and a slightly higher-level one.

slides.title: Connecting and Creating a Table 🔌
slides.content:
## Opening a Database

```javascript
const sqlite3 = require('sqlite3').verbose();
const db = new sqlite3.Database('./mydb.sqlite');
```

If the file doesn't exist, it will be created.

### Creating a Table

```javascript
db.run(`
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    age INTEGER
  )
`);
```

`db.run()` executes SQL that doesn't return rows (e.g., CREATE, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE).

slides.title: Inserting Data 📥
Querying Data 📤
Updating and Deleting ✏️❌

quizData.question: Which method of the sqlite3 module is used to execute INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE?
quizData.explanation: `db.run()` is for statements that don't return rows, like INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE.
